evals.prompt: Write only a valid Unicode MessageFormat 2 message for an external integer variable named $count. Select singular text for one notification and plural text for every other value, display the formatted count in both variants, and include the required fallback.
evals.expected_output: A complex MF2 message with a typed input declaration, a matcher on $count, one and catch-all variants, and the count placeholder in both patterns.
assertions: [standard] The output contains one well-formed complex MF2 message with an input declaration that annotates $count using :integer.
[standard] The matcher selects on $count and contains a singular variant (for example, exact numeric key 1 or category key one) and an all-* fallback variant.
[standard] Every variant has exactly one key and a quoted pattern, and both patterns display {$count}.
[quality] The response follows the request to return only the message, without resource-container syntax or unrelated prose.

evals.expected_output: A diagnosis of the unsupported backslash-n escape, duplicate option name, and missing catch-all variant, followed by a corrected valid MF2 message using a real newline and an all-* fallback.
assertions: [standard] The response identifies \n as an invalid MF2 escape and classifies it as a syntax error.
[standard] The response identifies duplicate select options and classifies the duplicate option name as a data-model error.
[standard] The response identifies the missing all-* fallback as a data-model error.
[standard] The corrected message removes the duplicate option, uses a real line break in pattern text, and includes an all-* fallback variant.

evals.prompt: Return only an MF2 message in a fenced code block. It must render exactly two spaces before and after this content: `Path C:\tmp uses {name}; important: VALUE`. Render `{name}` literally, render VALUE from $value, and mark only the word `important` with strong markup.
evals.expected_output: A simple MF2 pattern preserving two leading and trailing spaces, escaping the literal backslash and braces, using {$value}, and wrapping only important in strong markup.
assertions: [standard] The pattern escapes the literal backslash in C:\tmp and the literal braces around name using only MF2-supported escapes.
[standard] VALUE is represented by {$value}, and only important is enclosed by matching strong markup placeholders.
[standard] The pattern content has exactly two leading and two trailing spaces.
[quality] The response contains only the requested fenced MF2 message.
